findings.title: kcaH
findings.detail: Called by: AllocateFakeObjectNonPagedPool, AllocateFakeObjectNonPagedPoolNx, AllocateUaFObjectNonPagedPool, AllocateUaFObjectNonPagedPoolNx, CreateArbitraryReadWriteHelperObjectNonPagedPoolNx, DeleteArbitraryReadWriteHelperObjecNonPagedPoolNx, FreeUaFObjectNonPagedPool, FreeUaFObjectNonPagedPoolNx, TriggerBufferOverflowNonPagedPool, TriggerBufferOverflowNonPagedPoolNx, TriggerBufferOverflowPagedPoolSession, TriggerMemoryDisclosureNonPagedPool, TriggerMemoryDisclosureNonPagedPoolNx, TriggerNullPointerDereference, TriggerTypeConfusion, TriggerUninitializedMemoryPagedPool

findings.title: IoCreateDevice: world-accessible by default
findings.detail: IoCreateDevice assigns no security descriptor; accessible to all users. Consider IoCreateDeviceSecure with a restrictive SDDL.

findings.title: IoCreateSymbolicLink: \DosDevices\HackSysExtremeVulnerableDriver
findings.detail: Symbolic link exposes device to Win32 namespace; verify DACL on the target device object.

findings.title: Arbitrary write (write-what-where)
findings.detail: Store through a double-dereferenced (user-controlled) pointer `*(*p) = c`; verify the destination is validated

findings.title: Controlled pointer write
findings.detail: Value read through one pointer stored through another `*p = *q`; review whether the destination is attacker-controlled
